title: Bounty Arena as Licensable Product (Free for LB Members)
canonical_topic: The X-Ray Bounty Arena cluster packaged as a licensable SaaS product for non-LB sites, free for LB members, creating a triple flywheel of external revenue, cooperative conversion, and member retention.
composing_primitives: Embeddable widget / JavaScript SDK or Web Component
Triple flywheel (non-LB pays / non-LB converts / LB member gets free access)
SaaS license fee pricing model (per-site or per-active-user)
Guild Benefit Cascade: $5/year membership unlocks free BaaS
Three-currency Marks integration for external-site bounty hunters

title: HexIsle Modular Prototype: Bounty-to-Contract-to-Partnership Pipeline
canonical_topic: Meritocratic escalation pipeline for HexIsle makers from Bounty Hunter to Contractor to Partner based purely on accumulated Marks thresholds, enabling revenue share at 83.3% and Factory Node placement for the top tier.
composing_primitives: Three-tier escalation: Bounty Hunter (0-499) / Contractor (500) / Partner (2000+)
Marks-threshold gating replaces applications, interviews, and negotiations
Revenue share at 83.3% creator split for Partners
Half-life decay filters non-members from Partner track
Factory Node seed placement for 2000+ Mark Partners
founder_voice_quotes: The aggregate Marks threshold is COMPLETELY dependent on how much they contribute. Pure meritocracy through measurable contribution.

title: Canister System Launch Project + SlottedTop Co-Launch
canonical_topic: Canister System launched as a separate bounty-funded project alongside the SlottedTop Kickstarter (Campaign 1 of 13), plus a 'DO THE WORK = GET THE STATUS' Cue Card system as the recruitment mechanism for the bounty-to-partnership pipeline.
composing_primitives: Bounty-funded manufacturing pipeline covering every production stage
Separate project isolation (risk, audience, timeline) from HexIsle
1/3 funding model: pre-orders / LB treasury / production partner
DO THE WORK = GET THE STATUS recruitment Cue Card with QR to bounty board
5,000 Canister Systems + 15,000 SlottedTops co-launch production targets
founder_voice_quotes: DO THE WORK = GET THE STATUS. A teenager printing test pieces can reach Partner faster than an engineer who fills out a form and stops.

title: Canister System Bounty-Funded Manufacturing Project
canonical_topic: The Canister System as a vertically integrated bounty-funded manufacturing project where every production stage (CAD, printing, sourcing, mold design, assembly, QA, packaging, fulfillment) is a posted bounty, applying the Bounty-to-Partnership pipeline directly to hardware production.
composing_primitives: 8-stage vertically integrated bounty pipeline (CAD through fulfillment)
Stage-specific Marks ranges (50-200 for design, 10-50 for assembly)
5,000 Canister Systems production target with separate Pedestal/Project page
Bounty-to-Partnership pipeline applied to physical hardware manufacturing
C+20% cooperative retail at $249-499 vs. Morgan Press $1,200+
founder_voice_quotes: Your contribution IS your resume. The bounty ledger IS your interview. The revenue share IS your contract.

title: Desktop Injection Molder Factory Node Placement + Manufacturing Escalation Ladder (Three-Level Production Tier)
canonical_topic: Desktop electric injection molders (Babyplast class) co-funded at 1/3 model placed at Partner-level Factory Nodes, plus a formalized three-level production tier architecture (Canister/Desktop/Industrial) with forward-compatible mold design across all levels.
composing_primitives: Desktop molder placement at 2000+ Mark (Partner) Factory Nodes via 1/3 funding
Machine landscape: Babyplast 6/12T (~$15K-25K), APSX-PIM V3 (~$13.5K), MicroMolder (~$2-3K)
Three-level escalation: Canister (3D-printed molds) / Desktop (aluminum molds) / Industrial (steel molds)
Forward-compatible product design: Level 1 design re-tooled for Level 2 and Level 3
Marks ladder IS the manufacturing ladder — track record replaces credit score
founder_voice_quotes: No manufacturing network uses a gamified contribution system to determine who gets placed with increasingly expensive equipment.

title: SLS Production Node: When a Maker Becomes a Shop
canonical_topic: SLS machine placement as the Level 3 threshold in the manufacturing escalation ladder, transforming a maker with a bench into a shop capable of producing engineering-grade parts for any customer from any STL without molds, at the 2000+ Marks Partner tier with 1/3 cooperative funding.
composing_primitives: SLS as Level 3 of four-level manufacturing escalation (Canister/Desktop/SLS/Industrial)
No molds required: direct powder printing from customer STL files
1/3 funding model: Sinterit Lisa Pro ~$5K/party, Formlabs Fuse 1+ ~$8.3K/party
Shop business model: 83.3% split on LB marketplace orders + open-market capability
Three capabilities (Canister + Babyplast + SLS) under one roof = full manufacturing shop
founder_voice_quotes: A maker with a Canister System makes THEIR parts. A maker with a Babyplast makes parts at VOLUME. A maker with an SLS machine makes parts for OTHER PEOPLE. That's a shop.

title: Project-Entity Architecture + Multi-Vendor Prototype Validation
canonical_topic: Every LB platform project requires a business entity (min. sole prop), enabling LB to remain pure cooperative infrastructure while project-entities handle manufacturing and SEC-clean contractor payments; simultaneously, multiple bounty completers can compete as vendors selling their own versions of the same prototype, plus the Founder's LLC-per-product-line portfolio.
composing_primitives: Project-entity requirement (sole prop minimum, LLC recommended) for all projects
LB as cooperative infrastructure: marketplace / payment rails / patent portfolio / reputation
Multi-vendor prototype validation: live A/B market test with real customer revenue
Cash (LB Card) + Marks (cooperative) as two separate payment streams
Founder LLC-per-product-line portfolio (HexIsle LLC, Canister Manufacturing LLC) under LB Corp
founder_voice_quotes: 1,000 projects = 1,000 business entities, each responsible for their own operations. LB doesn't need to manage 1,000 product lines — it manages the PLATFORM.

title: $100 Business Starter Kit Sponsorship (10,000 Units, Patent-Backed)
canonical_topic: 10,000 Business Starter Kits at $100 each ($1M total) collateralized by the patent portfolio and distributed through maker spaces and veteran organizations, each converting a recipient into a business owner with membership, starter Marks, LB Card with $50 cash, and a DO THE WORK Cue Card.
composing_primitives: $1M kit program backed by 10 provisional applications (~1,511 formal claims)
Kit contents: $5 membership + formation guide + Cue Card QR + 500 starter Marks + LB Card ($50) + DO THE WORK physical card
Distribution: maker spaces, FabLabs, community colleges, veterans organizations, workforce development
HEOHO (Interdependence) page placement: 'We don't give people jobs. We give them businesses.'
C+20% margin payback through products built by every business started from a kit
founder_voice_quotes: Every member who starts a project becomes a BUSINESS OWNER. Even at sole prop level. This is the 'save the world' part.

title: A&A Formal Registration — Innovations #2098-#2099 (Lightning Bug Ideation Pipeline + Cooperative AI Knowledge Architecture)
canonical_topic: Two Crown Jewel candidates registered at B045: Lightning Bug (#2098) — a six-stage Marks-weighted idea metamorphosis from Ember to Lightning Strike that auto-graduates funded projects at 1000+ Marks; and Librarian Guild (#2099) — a hierarchical cooperative AI knowledge architecture with 5-7 Section Librarians, dual Master Librarians, and a Red Queen verification layer solving the context-window ceiling problem.

1000+ Marks threshold auto-graduates idea to funded project with backer originator registry
5-7 domain-specialized Section Librarians producing dual output (human narrative + JSON catalog)
Dual independent Master Librarians with Red Queen comparator for hallucination/drift prevention
Machine-readable Catalog queryable by any agent (Knight/Bishop/Rook/Pawn) without full context ingestion

title: A&A Formal Registration — Innovations #2100-#2105 (B049 Batch)
canonical_topic: Six innovations registered in Bishop B049: Bounty Photography Network (zero-storage metadata), Pearl Diver Resource Intelligence (physical-world deal discovery), Universal Member Subscriptions (four-currency recurring channels), Cooperative Classroom (home teaching dual revenue), Cue Card Pioneer Program (diminishing-reward role validation), and Freezer Node (cooperative batch meal prep hub).

Pearl Diver dual-track: Quiet Pearl (Resource Board only) vs Pearl Influencer (social + board)
Universal subscriptions: any member, four currencies at permanent parity, 83.3% creator retention
Cooperative Classroom: Zoom delivery + platform commerce, 83.3% vs. Udemy's 37%
Cue Card Pioneer Program: diminishing-reward tiers (Founders Circle 50 Marks/mo → Standard at 1000 practitioners)

title: Bounty Photography Network: Zero-Storage Visual Documentation Marketplace for Cooperative Commerce
canonical_topic: Dual-channel cooperative photography marketplace where members photograph local businesses posting to personal social media (platform stores only ~850 bytes metadata), AND businesses post their own recurring photography bounties through the same system, enabling a full-time cooperative photography career path from casual to Photography Captain.
composing_primitives: Zero-storage architecture: social media IS the storage layer, platform is a content registrar
Channel 1: community-initiated platform bounties (2 Marks/photo, +5 if on Treasure Map, +10 if leads to onboarding)
Channel 2: business-initiated bounties for recurring product/event/listing photography at Cost+20%
Real estate vertical as killer app: agent listing photos at 50-67% below market rate, on-demand same-day
Career ladder: Casual Photographer → Active → Professional → Guild → Photography Captain ($5K-8K/mo)

title: Response Rate Analytics — Optimal Contact Window Intelligence System
canonical_topic: Cooperative platform analytics system measuring optimal outbound contact timing across seven dimensions (day of week, hour, day of month, week of year, channel, content type, recipient type) served as a trust-transparent member tool rather than advertiser-serving extraction.
composing_primitives: 7-dimension timing analysis with Bayesian continuous updating
Three-audience surfacing: platform business intelligence / Captain tool / cooperative governance
Privacy-preserving: no cookies or pixel tracking, individual data never exposed
Optimizes for actual response/conversion, not engagement/likes
$5/year membership includes analytics vs. $15-300/month for Buffer/Hootsuite

title: A&A #2101 ADDENDUM — Pearl Diver Subscription Model + Visual Identity
canonical_topic: Addendum to Pearl Diver (#2101) formalizing four subscription tiers (Free/Basic 3cr/Premium 5cr/VIP 10cr), quality-gated eligibility requirements (30+ verified tips, 85% accuracy, ADAPT Silver+), city-wide Pearl Diver Network guild subscription bundles, and brass deep-sea diving helmet visual identity with flashlight beam and pearl.
composing_primitives: Four subscription tiers: Free / Basic 3cr / Premium 5cr / VIP 10cr per month
Quality gates: 30+ verified tips, 85% accuracy rate, 30+ days active, ADAPT Silver+
City-wide guild subscription bundle (e.g., San Antonio Pearl Diver Network, 10 Credits/month)
Brass Mark V deep-sea helmet icon with golden-white flashlight beam revealing pearl
Subscription math: 100 Premium subscribers = 416.50 Credits/month to Pearl Diver

title: Pearl Diver Resource Intelligence Network: Community-Sourced Deal Discovery with Optional Influencer Track
canonical_topic: Two-track cooperative deal intelligence system where Pearl Divers log physical-world deal intelligence (store discount schedules, stacking combinations, clearance timing, hidden sources) to a cooperative Resource Board, with an optional influencer track posting to social media, and verified tips feeding directly into Let's Get Groceries cooperative group purchasing.
composing_primitives: Track 1: Quiet Pearl — Resource Board only, no audience required (2-15 Marks per tip type)
Track 2: Pearl Influencer — Resource Board + social media, additional attribution Marks
8 tip categories: store deal / stacking discount / schedule intel / hidden source / seasonal alert / price comparison / meal deal / bulk buy trigger
Let's Get Groceries integration: verified tips trigger cooperative group purchases
Pearl Diver Guild for cross-city deal network with ADAPT scoring (accuracy, consistency, transmission)
founder_voice_quotes: The knowledge Diana Jones carries in her head is worth thousands of dollars per year to people who don't have it.

title: A&A #2102 ADDENDUM 2 — All-Currency Payment + TasteMaker Integration
canonical_topic: Addendum to Universal Member Subscriptions extending payment to all four cooperative currencies at permanent parity (1 Mark = 1 Credit = 1 Joule = $1), plus TasteMaker-verified subscription channels (SAA 500+) as an earned trust signal for project-recommendation subscriptions — with ONE LEVEL attribution only.
composing_primitives: All-currency parity: Marks / Credits / Joules / Dollars all at 1:1:1:1
Internal ledger advantage: three cooperative currencies process at zero Stripe fees
TasteMaker badge (SAA 500+) earned — not claimed — displayed on subscription channels
ONE LEVEL rule: TasteMaker earns subscription revenue, NOT commission from subscriber-backed projects
Triple-stack career model: Photographer + Pearl Diver + TasteMaker = full-time cooperative income

title: Universal Member Subscription System: Dual-Currency Recurring Channel Subscriptions for Every Cooperative Member
canonical_topic: Platform-wide subscription engine where any $5/year member creates subscription channels with subscribers choosing to pay in Marks (internal zero-fee ledger) or dollars (Stripe recurring), both at Cost+20% with 83.3% creator retention, creating a self-reinforcing Marks economy flywheel.
composing_primitives: Any member can create subscriptions — no follower minimum, no monetization threshold, no creator tier
Dual-currency: Marks via internal ledger (zero Stripe fees) OR dollars via Stripe recurring
Creator sets both prices; subscriber chooses currency per billing cycle
Marks economy flywheel: value circulates member-to-member without real money leaving the cooperative
Constitutional margin lock: no board/investor/governance action can change the 83.3% creator retention

title: Cooperative Classroom: Home-Based Teaching Marketplace with Dual Revenue (Subscription Classes + Individual Sessions)
canonical_topic: Home-based teaching marketplace where any qualified member teaches live classes via Zoom at 83.3% earnings — 2.25x more per $1K/month than Udemy — with dual revenue streams (subscription group classes + per-session individual tutoring), four-currency payment, and Teacher Guild formation, connecting to the Didasko initiative.
composing_primitives: Dual revenue: subscription group classes (up to 15 students) + per-session individual tutoring
Zero video hosting: Zoom delivers instruction, platform handles scheduling/billing/discovery
Four-currency payment: Marks / Credits / Joules / Dollars via #2102 Universal Subscriptions
83.3% creator share vs. Udemy 37%, Preply 67% — $5,556/year more at $1K/month level
Teachers Guild with benefit cascade: shared curriculum (5) → group Zoom licenses (25) → guild-level bundles (100)

title: Cue Card Pioneer Program: Diminishing-Reward Early Adopter System for Cooperative Role Validation
canonical_topic: TasteTester-pattern diminishing-reward pioneer program applying to every Cue Card role, where first 10 pioneers (Founders' Circle) receive 50 Marks/month for 12 months plus a serial-numbered physical Pioneer Medallion with QR code in exchange for consenting to real-name case study marketing, with rewards tapering to zero at 1,000 practitioners when the role is statistically proven.
composing_primitives: Four-tier pioneer structure: Founders' Circle (1-10, 50 Marks/mo 12mo) / Trailblazer (11-100) / Pathfinder (101-500) / Early Adopter (501-1000)
Physical Pioneer Medallion: brass, serial-numbered (PIONEER-TEACHER-001), QR to pioneer showcase page
Real-name case study opt-in for Founders' Circle — 'Rosario earns $1,200/month' recruits next 100 teachers
1,000-practitioner statistical validation threshold: real averages, real failure data, media-citable
Self-extending system: every new Cue Card role automatically receives Pioneer Program infrastructure
founder_voice_quotes: Every new Cue Card role is unproven until someone does it. These pioneers take the RISK of being first. That risk deserves reward.

title: Freezer Node: Community Meal Prep, Storage, and Distribution Hub for Cooperative Food Systems
canonical_topic: Cooperative food production micro-business where members batch-cook meals using cooperative-sourced recipes and bulk ingredients, freeze in portioned containers, and sell via subscription and on-demand orders at Cost+20% (83.3% cook retention), applying the Factory Node micro-production model to food.
composing_primitives: Batch cook → portion → freeze → list → subscription/on-demand distribution model
Let's Get Groceries + Pearl Diver deal alerts reduce ingredient costs 20-40%
Subscription meal plans (3/5/10 meals/week) powered by #2102 Universal Subscriptions
Startup cost: $55-105 + one Sunday (vs. $100K-500K restaurant)
Marginal cooking economics: 20 servings costs ~40% more than 4 servings but generates 4x revenue

#2110 is a proposed backfill for gap left by reverted K162 migration
